user_query: Predict what systematic differences you expect between the GGA/GGA+U vs R2SCAN pretrained CHGNet models' predictions (after relaxation) for density and volume per atom compared to experimental values, and then prove your hypothesis with data and plots. You should use all the ICSD structures provided at the dataset path read from os.getenv('ICSD_DATA_PATH'). Before each relaxation, apply a uniform strain that decreases all lattice vectors by 1%. Set the maximum force tolerance for relaxation to 30 meV/Å.
solution_code_or_process: Use the code files in gga_r2scan_density_and_volume_chgnet/solution_code folder. Remember to set the environment variable ICSD_DATA_PATH to yourabsolutepath/deep_solver_benchmark/data_for_demonstration/icsd_structure.json and source ~/.bashrc, so the agents can load the dataset given by the user query. First run the gga_relax.py and r2scan_relax.py to get the icsd_relaxation_gga.json and icsd_relaxation_r2scan.json, then run the plot_distributions.py to get two density and volume per atom distribution plots. From these plots, you can see that GGA tends to overestimate volumes and underestimate densities compared to R2SCAN.
notes: All the contents in the agent_answer_o3 folder's files are automatically generated by the agents of OpenAI o3 model. During testing, the paper “Cross-functional transferability in foundation machine learning interatomic potentials” was unpublished (which includes a section about density predictions), which reduced publicly available hints for the agent.
